message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>In this article, we are going to show how to find out short term forecasts of the total number of COVID-19 cases in India in an easy way. Initially the spread of the disease was observably slow in India. Since the first week of May a highly nonlinear pattern has started to take shape. It can be observed that currently in India the spread pattern is nearly exponential. It can be seen further that the number of cases is still continuing to grow very fast. Therefore, instead of going for rigorous time series analysis, we may opt for looking at the data from a recent date downwards, and short term forecasts based on simple numerical analytical methods can be made accordingly.</jats:p>
title: The Current COVID-19 Spread Pattern in India

reference.unstructured: P. Kumar , R. K. Singh , C. Nanda . et. al. Forecasting COVID-19 impact in India using pandemic waves nonlinear growth models, medRxiv preprint doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.03.30.2004703.
N. Poonia , S. Azad , Short term forecasts of COVID-19 spread across Indian States until May 1, 2020, arXiv: 2004.13538v2[q.bio.PE ].

S. Azad , N. Poonia , Short term forecasts of COVID-19 spread across Indian States until 29 May, 2020, under the worst case scenario, Preprints 202000491. https://doi.org/10.20944/preprints202004.0491.v1
P. Ghosh , R. Ghosh , B. Chakraborty , COVID-19 in India: State-wise analysis and prediction, medRxiv preprint, doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.04.24.20077792.
K. Tseng , E. Schueller , G. Kapoor , et. al. Center for Disease Dynamics, Economics & Policy, Washington, DC, COVID-19 for India updates, March 24, 2020.

reference.article-title: Prediction for the spread of COVID-19 in India and effectiveness of preventive measures
